content.body: Caio Lima: re your example. I didn't think it through in-depth but I agree with holger. The interesting bit is that a debugger can not even pause on the `25`. At most it would pause on the `console.log` call. The `25` itself can also not cause a JS Error (with a stack trace) unless it's a syntax error and those don't have a stack. So whatever info terser emits there would be ignored by most debuggers anyway, at which point terser can skip emitting range info for trivial things like literals.
